message:
perf(persist): cache short-lived ephemeral writer + memoize ensure_source per conversation

Two related optimizations to the hot persist path that
`with_ephemeral_writer(...)` drives during ingestion. Both are scoped
to a single FrankenStorage instance and committed together because
the source-cache is invalidated through the same storage handle that
owns the cached writer.

## 1. Cached ephemeral writer (FrankenStorage::cached_ephemeral_writer)

Previously every `with_ephemeral_writer(storage, defer, ctx, f)` call
opened a fresh frankensqlite writer connection at `db_path`, applied
PRAGMAs, ran the preflight write probe, executed `f`, then closed the
connection. For an ingestion run that persists thousands of small
conversations one at a time, the open+pragma+close cycle dominated
wall time even though no other writer ever raced.

Storage now keeps a `parking_lot::Mutex<CachedEphemeralWriter>` with
three states:
  - `Uninitialized` — no cached writer; open one
  - `Cached(Box<SendFrankenConnection>)` — idle reusable writer
  - `InUse` — checked out; reentrant calls fall back to a fresh
    one-shot writer to preserve the previous concurrency model

`acquire_cached_ephemeral_writer()` moves the cache to InUse and
returns `(FrankenStorage, reusable: bool)` to the caller. The caller's
release path branches on `reusable`:
  - true → `release_cached_ephemeral_writer(writer)` returns the
    connection to the cache for the next call
  - false → `writer.close()` runs the original close path

Error paths (preflight failure, `f` returning Err) call
`discard_cached_ephemeral_writer` so a poisoned connection isn't
returned to the cache.

Connection lifecycle hooks (`close`, `close_without_checkpoint`,
`close_best_effort_in_place`, `close_without_checkpoint_in_place`,
`into_raw`) all flush the cache so a cached writer can never outlive
its parent storage handle.

New regression test
`with_ephemeral_writer_reuses_cached_connection_when_idle` proves
reuse: a TEMP TABLE created on the first call survives into the
second `with_ephemeral_writer(...)` invocation, which is only
possible if the underlying connection was reused.

## 2. Memoized ensure_source for single-conversation inserts

`insert_conversation_tree` and the batch path both call
`ensure_source_for_conversation`, which used to issue an autocommit
`upsert_source` per conversation even when nothing about the source
had changed since the last insert.

Storage now carries an
`Arc<Mutex<HashSet<EnsuredConversationSourceKey>>>` shared across
clones (so the cached writer + the parent storage see the same set).
`EnsuredConversationSourceKey` keys on `(id, kind, host_label)` —
the fields the source row materially depends on for ingestion.

`ensure_source_for_conversation` now skips the upsert when the key
is already in the set, and `ensure_sources_for_batch` populates the
set after each upsert. `upsert_source` and `delete_source`
invalidate the cache for the affected source id so any out-of-band
mutation forces re-upsert on the next conversation insert.

## 3. Test-only stage profilers (instrumentation, not behavior)

Both files gain `#[cfg(test)]` perf profilers that break down a
persist call into named stages (db / packet / positional /
tantivy_add for indexer; source / tx_open / message_insert /
snippet_insert / fts_entry / fts_flush / analytics / commit for
storage) and emit a single `eprintln!` line tagged
`CASS_PERSIST_STAGE_PROFILE` / `CASS_INSERT_TREE_STAGE_PROFILE` so
benchmarks can grep for steady-state numbers without hand-rolling
timers. Two `#[serial]` tests
(`persist_conversation_stage_profile_tracks_steady_state_remote_reuse`,
`insert_conversation_tree_stage_profile_tracks_steady_state_remote_reuse`)
exercise the writer-reuse path and source-cache hit path together.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
